bmr_mifflin_st_jeor_men.name: Mifflin-St Jeor (Men)
bmr_mifflin_st_jeor_men.description: Standard best practice BMR equation for men. Most accurate for normal weight individuals.
bmr_mifflin_st_jeor_men.compute: lambda weight_kg, height_cm, age: round((10 * weight_kg) + (6.25 * height_cm) - (5 * age) + 5, 1)
bmr_mifflin_st_jeor_men.conditions: Use when weight, height, and age are known but body fat % is not.

bmr_mifflin_st_jeor_women.name: Mifflin-St Jeor (Women)
bmr_mifflin_st_jeor_women.description: Standard best practice BMR equation for women.
bmr_mifflin_st_jeor_women.compute: lambda weight_kg, height_cm, age: round((10 * weight_kg) + (6.25 * height_cm) - (5 * age) - 161, 1)
bmr_mifflin_st_jeor_women.conditions: Use when weight, height, and age are known but body fat % is not.

bmr_katch_mcardle.name: Katch-McArdle
bmr_katch_mcardle.description: Most accurate BMR equation when lean body mass is known. Independent of body fat %.
bmr_katch_mcardle.compute: lambda weight_kg, body_fat_pct: round(370 + (21.6 * weight_kg * (1 - body_fat_pct/100)), 1)
bmr_katch_mcardle.conditions: Use when body fat % is available. More accurate than Mifflin-St Jeor.

bmr_harris_benedict_men.name: Harris-Benedict Revised (Men)
bmr_harris_benedict_men.description: Legacy BMR equation for men (Roza & Shizgal, 1984).
bmr_harris_benedict_men.compute: lambda weight_kg, height_cm, age: round(88.362 + (13.397 * weight_kg) + (4.799 * height_cm) - (5.677 * age), 1)
bmr_harris_benedict_men.conditions: Legacy equation, less accurate than Mifflin-St Jeor for modern populations.

bmr_harris_benedict_women.name: Harris-Benedict Revised (Women)
bmr_harris_benedict_women.description: Legacy BMR equation for women (Roza & Shizgal, 1984).
bmr_harris_benedict_women.compute: lambda weight_kg, height_cm, age: round(447.593 + (9.247 * weight_kg) + (3.098 * height_cm) - (4.330 * age), 1)
bmr_harris_benedict_women.conditions: Legacy equation, less accurate than Mifflin-St Jeor for modern populations.

tdee.name: Total Daily Energy Expenditure (TDEE)
tdee.description: TDEE = BMR x Activity Multiplier. Estimates total calories burned per day.
tdee.conditions: Use after calculating BMR to estimate total daily calorie needs.

one_rm_epley.name: 1RM Estimation (Epley)
one_rm_epley.description: Estimates one-rep max from submaximal sets. Best for reps <= 10.
one_rm_epley.conditions: Best for reps <= 10. Tends to overestimate for higher reps.

one_rm_brzycki.name: 1RM Estimation (Brzycki)
one_rm_brzycki.description: Most accurate 1RM estimation for reps up to 10. Widely used in powerlifting.
one_rm_brzycki.conditions: Highly accurate up to 10 reps. Most coaches prefer this formula.

one_rm_lander.name: 1RM Estimation (Lander)
one_rm_lander.description: Alternative 1RM estimation formula, slightly more conservative than Epley.
one_rm_lander.compute: lambda weight, reps: round((100 * weight) / (101.3 - 2.6712 * reps), 1)
one_rm_lander.conditions: Good alternative to Brzycki, slightly more conservative.

macro_protein_target.name: Daily Protein Target
macro_protein_target.description: Calculates daily protein target based on body weight and training goal.

inputs.name: goal
inputs.description: Training goal: general_health, endurance, muscle_building, or cutting

macro_protein_target.formula: protein_g = weight_kg * multiplier (0.8 general, 1.2-1.4 endurance, 1.6-2.2 muscle, 2.0-2.4 cut)
macro_protein_target.conditions: Adjust multiplier based on goal and training intensity.

macro_calorie_targets.name: Calorie Targets by Goal
macro_calorie_targets.description: Calculates daily calorie targets based on TDEE and fitness goal.
macro_calorie_targets.formula: cut: TDEE - 300 to 700; bulk: TDEE + 200 to 500; maintain: TDEE +/- 100
macro_calorie_targets.compute: lambda tdee, goal: {"fat_loss": round(tdee - 500, 0), "maintain": round(tdee, 0), "muscle_gain": round(tdee + 300, 0)}.get(goal, round(tdee, 0))
macro_calorie_targets.conditions: Fat loss: 300-700 cal deficit. Muscle gain: 200-500 cal surplus.

body_fat_navy_men.name: Body Fat % (Navy Formula, Men)
body_fat_navy_men.description: Estimates body fat percentage using waist and neck circumferences (US Navy method).
body_fat_navy_men.compute: lambda waist_cm, neck_cm, height_cm: round(495 / (1.0324 - 0.19077 * __import__("math").log10(waist_cm - neck_cm) + 0.15456 * __import__("math").log10(height_cm)) - 450, 1)
body_fat_navy_men.conditions: Accuracy +/- 3-4% compared to DEXA. Consistent measurement is key.

body_fat_navy_women.name: Body Fat % (Navy Formula, Women)
body_fat_navy_women.description: Estimates body fat percentage using waist, hip, and neck circumferences.
body_fat_navy_women.compute: lambda waist_cm, hip_cm, neck_cm, height_cm: round(495 / (1.29579 - 0.35004 * __import__("math").log10(waist_cm + hip_cm - neck_cm) + 0.22100 * __import__("math").log10(height_cm)) - 450, 1)
body_fat_navy_women.conditions: Accuracy +/- 3-4% compared to DEXA. Consistent measurement is key.

bmi.name: Body Mass Index (BMI)
bmi.description: Simple weight-to-height ratio. Does not account for muscle mass.
bmi.conditions: Screening tool only. Does not differentiate muscle from fat. Not accurate for muscular individuals.

acwr.name: Acute:Chronic Workload Ratio (ACWR)
acwr.description: Measures training load relative to fitness. Key injury prevention metric from Gabbett et al.

inputs.name: acute_volume
inputs.description: This week's total volume load (sets x reps x weight)

inputs.name: chronic_volume
inputs.description: Average weekly volume over past 4 weeks

acwr.compute: lambda acute_volume, chronic_volume: round(acute_volume / chronic_volume, 2) if chronic_volume > 0 else None

karvonen_thr.name: Target Heart Rate (Karvonen)
karvonen_thr.description: Heart rate reserve method for target heart rate zones. More accurate than %MHR alone.
karvonen_thr.compute: lambda max_hr, resting_hr, intensity_pct: round(((max_hr - resting_hr) * intensity_pct) + resting_hr, 0)
karvonen_thr.conditions: More accurate than simple %maxHR because it accounts for fitness level via resting HR.

max_hr_fox.name: Max Heart Rate (Fox/Haskell)
max_hr_fox.description: Simple age-based maximum heart rate estimation.
max_hr_fox.conditions: Standard formula. Tanaka (208-0.7xAge) is more accurate for older adults.

max_hr_tanaka.name: Max Heart Rate (Tanaka)
max_hr_tanaka.description: More accurate age-based MHR formula, especially for older adults.
max_hr_tanaka.conditions: More accurate than Fox formula, especially for adults over 40.

volume_load.name: Volume Load (Tonnage)
volume_load.description: Total training volume: sets x reps x weight. Fundamental metric for tracking progress.
volume_load.compute: lambda sets, reps, weight: round(sets * reps * weight, 1)
volume_load.conditions: Use for tracking total workout volume. Compare week-to-week for progressive overload.

irv.name: Intensity Relative Volume (IRV)
irv.description: Volume adjusted for intensity: sets x reps x %1RM. Optimal range 20-30 per exercise.
irv.compute: lambda sets, reps, pct_1rm: round(sets * reps * pct_1rm, 1)
irv.conditions: Optimal range: 20-30 per exercise for hypertrophy. Below 20 = undertraining, above 30 = overreaching.
